task_completion.description: LibreCAD is open and the required layers are created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window is visible. The Layer List panel shows custom layers including OUTLINE, LEDGER, JOISTS, BEAM, POSTS, STAIRS, and DIMENSIONS.

task_completion.description: The main deck outline and ledger board are drawn
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large vertical rectangle is visible in the drawing area representing the 144x192 deck. A line or double line is visible at the top edge of this rectangle representing the ledger board.

task_completion.description: The beam line and three post markers are drawn
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal line bisects the deck outline across the middle (Y=96). Three distinct markers (e.g., small circles, crosses, or points) are placed along this middle beam line at the left edge, center, and right edge.

task_completion.description: Vertical joist lines are drawn across the deck
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple evenly spaced vertical lines fill the main deck outline from left to right (approximately 10 lines spaced 16 units apart).

task_completion.description: The stair rectangle is drawn centered at the bottom edge
task_completion.visual_evidence: A smaller rectangle (36x48 units) is attached to the bottom center of the main deck outline.

task_completion.description: Dimensions are added to the drawing
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines with text are visible, indicating the overall width, overall length, and the spacing between the joists.

task_completion.description: The file is saved as deck_framing_plan.dxf
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'deck_framing_plan.dxf' or a save dialog is visible confirming the file is being saved with this name in the Documents/LibreCAD directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD interface with active drawing tools, layer panel interactions, and cursor movements in the canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal script to generate the DXF file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open running Python or bash scripts designed to programmatically generate the DXF file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write DXF code in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code) are open containing raw DXF format text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'deck_framing_plan.dxf'. The deck dimensions are 144 units wide by 192 units long, with joists spaced 16 units apart (resulting in about 10 vertical joist lines). The drawing must contain 7 specific layers: OUTLINE, LEDGER, JOISTS, BEAM, POSTS, STAIRS, and DIMENSIONS.
